description_long: This imposing clock tower is constructed from dark, textured stone blocks, giving it a fortress-like appearance reminiscent of medieval architecture. The structure features two tall, rectangular towers with crenellated tops and narrow, square windows, flanking a central section that houses a large, circular clock face with Roman numerals and simple clock hands. The main entrance is an arched, wooden double door set into the base of the central section, directly beneath the clock. The side wings of the building are lower and also topped with crenellations, adding to the fortified look. The overall form is symmetrical, with the clock face serving as the focal point on the front facade. The exterior is predominantly dark gray, with the clock face and door providing lighter visual accents.
description: A dark stone clock tower with twin crenellated towers, a large Roman numeral clock face, and a central arched entrance. The structure is fortress-like and symmetrical, with medieval architectural features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin towers, large clock face with Roman numerals, and central arched entrance.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure, crenellated roofline, and the thickness of the stone walls.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical, blocky back of the building with crenellations and no visible clock face or entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the side wall, crenellations, and the rear tower structure.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is large, circular, and light beige in color, featuring black Roman numerals evenly spaced around the perimeter. The clock hands are simple and dark, with no ornate or decorative elements. The face is set into the stone facade and is clearly visible from the front.
